perturbation.original_text: (b) The parties acknowledge and agree that all Confidential Information is provided “AS -IS” and without any warranty, express, implied or otherwise, regarding such Confidential Infor mation’s accuracy or completeness. Each party understands and agrees that neither party shall have any liability whatsoever to the other party relating to or resulting from such party’s use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) The parties acknowledge and agree that all Confidential Information is provided “AS -IS” and with full warranty, express and implied, regarding such Confidential Information’s accuracy and completeness. Each party understands and agrees that both parties shall have full liability to the other party relating to or resulting from such party’s use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by directly negating the original clause regarding warranties and liability. Initially, the agreement stated 'no warranty' and 'no liability'. The modified text states 'full warranty' and 'full liability'. This creates a direct conflict, rendering the enforceability uncertain and potentially leading to legal disputes about the extent of warranty and liability for the use of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) The parties acknowledge and agree that all Confidential Information is provided “AS -IS” and with full warranty, express and implied, regarding such Confidential Information’s accuracy and completeness. Each party understands and agrees that both parties shall have full liability to the other party relating to or resulting from such party’s use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by replacing 'no warranty' with 'full warranty' and 'no liability' with 'full liability'. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, indicating that the change introduces a direct conflict within the document regarding warranty and liability for confidential information. This fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type as it creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: (e) Upon the disclosing party’s request , all documents, materials and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information (including without limitation, all originals, copies, and reproductions of the Confidential Information) shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party without retaining a copy thereof, an d the receiving party shall provide a certification , signed by an officer, as to the completeness of the return of such material s.
perturbation.changed_text: (e) Upon the disclosing party’s request, all documents, materials and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information (including without limitation, all originals, copies, and reproductions of the Confidential Information) shall be destroyed and certified as such, and the receiving party shall retain a copy thereof and the original request.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause dictates that all confidential information is returned to the disclosing party without the receiving party retaining a copy. The modified clause states that the information is to be destroyed and that the receiving party should retain a copy. This directly contradicts the previous instruction, introducing uncertainty in the required action and making it unclear whether the information should be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) Upon the disclosing party’s request, all documents, materials and other items containing the disclosing party’s Confidential Information (including without limitation, all originals, copies, and reproductions of the Confidential Information) shall be destroyed and certified as such, and the receiving party shall retain a copy thereof and the original request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon the disclosing party's request, all confidential information must be returned without the receiving party retaining a copy. The changed text, which is located in the same area as the contradicted text, dictates that the information is to be destroyed, but the receiving party retains a copy. This direct contradiction in required actions (returning vs. destroying and not retaining vs. retaining a copy) creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. (a) Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing , directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties , and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party in trust and strict confidence, subject to the terms of this Agreement ; (ii) take reasonable security precautions, at least as great as the precautions it takes to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care, to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information and avoid d isclosure, loss or misuse of the Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. (a) Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties, and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party without any confidentiality obligations; (ii) take no security precautions to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation directly contradicts the core confidentiality obligations. It states that the receiving party holds the information 'without any confidentiality obligations' and should 'take no security precautions.' This conflicts with the entire premise of the NDA, creating an in-text contradiction about whether confidentiality is actually required. The enforceability of the agreement is severely undermined, leading to disputes over the responsibility to protect confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Obligations Regarding Confidential Information. (a) Subject to the Tennessee Public Records Act, Section 10 -7-5-1 et seq., a t all times during the term of this Agreement and thereafter, the receiving party shall: (i) refrain from disclosing, directly or indirectly, any Confidential Information of the disclosing party to third parties, and hold all Confidential Information received from the disclosing party without any confidentiality obligations; (ii) take no security precautions to keep confidential the disclosing party’s Confidential Information; and (iii) refrain from using any of the disclosing party’s Confidential Information for any purpose not specified her eunder (including , without limitation , in its own business or for its own benefit) without the prior written consent of the disclosing p arty.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the core confidentiality obligations outlined in the original text and reinforced in the contradicted text. The changed text explicitly states that the receiving party holds confidential information 'without any confidentiality obligations' and should 'take no security precautions.' This directly clashes with the fundamental purpose of a confidentiality agreement and creates a clear and undeniable contradiction within the document. This is because the original text (and premise of a NDA) implies confidentiality should be held and that precautions should be taken, while the changed text completely removes those obligations. Therefore, the change introduced a strong in-text contradiction based on the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (a) For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means any and all information (including , without limitation, all business, financial, commerci al and technical information and data) , in whatever form transmitted, whether tangible or intangible, that is disclosed, furnished or otherwise provided or made available by the d isclosing p arty to the receiving p arty prior to or after the date of this Agreement . “Confidential Information” includes, without limitation, design documents, trade secrets, files, marketing and business plans, documentation, reports, projections, interpretations, forecasts, records (including information regarding the disclosing party’s businesses, customers, strategies, operations, finances, technology, data, processes, methodologies, know -how, existing or future products, services, applications and methods of operation (including how such methods are developed, conducted or operated)) and all other information, whether in written, oral, encoded, electronic or other tangible or intangible form, and whether or not labeled, marked or otherwise identified as “Confidential” upon disclosure thereof, relating to the disclosing party’s products and businesses , and any other information that would, if disclosed to any actual or potential competitors of the disclosing party , give or increase those competitors’ advantage over the d isclosing party or directly or indirectly harm the disclosin g party’s business.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means only information explicitly marked as “Confidential” at the time of disclosure. All other information, regardless of its nature or sensitivity, will not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a significant in-text contradiction by drastically narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information.' The original definition was broad, encompassing various types of information regardless of labeling. The modified definition restricts it to only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential.' This contradicts other sections of the agreement that assume a broader definition of 'Confidential Information,' leading to uncertainty about what information is protected and enforceable under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means only information explicitly marked as “Confidential” at the time of disclosure. All other information, regardless of its nature or sensitivity, will not be considered Confidential Information under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include information explicitly marked as such. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which assumes a broader definition. Since both clauses are in the same location, this signifies a large change and contradiction, making it unclear which definition applies and creating a direct conflict within the document regarding the scope of confidentiality.
